title: Chronic checkup
story: A health check and medicine purchase became unavoidable this month.
knowledgePoint: Medical shocks are low-frequency but high-impact, so reserves and protection matter.
teacherNote: Explain emergency savings and insurance as two layers of protection.

title: Flu and missed work
story: You lose a few working days because of a flu episode.
knowledgePoint: Health events can hurt both income and expenses at the same time.
teacherNote: Use this to explain why liquidity matters even without a large hospital bill.

title: Dental treatment
story: A delayed dental problem now requires treatment and medicine.
knowledgePoint: Delaying small care needs often makes the bill larger later.
teacherNote: Contrast prevention spending with emergency spending.

title: Minor injury treatment
story: A sports or commute injury leads to scans and physical therapy.
knowledgePoint: Accident insurance is for low-probability but painful losses.
teacherNote: Use this to compare health cover with accident cover.

title: Mental health support
story: You pay for a counseling session after a stressful month.
knowledgePoint: Mental health also affects financial decision quality and resilience.
teacherNote: Link stress, impulsive choices, and risk tolerance.

title: Family medical transfer
story: A family member needs urgent support and you transfer money home.
knowledgePoint: Family obligations are part of real-life financial planning.
teacherNote: Discuss caregiving, family support, and why reserves should not only cover the self.

title: Broken water heater
story: The landlord covers part of it, but you still pay for the repair.
knowledgePoint: Housing costs include maintenance and surprise spending, not just rent.
teacherNote: Good example for fixed cost pressure.

title: Forced move
story: A sudden move means paying transport, deposits, and setup costs.
knowledgePoint: Moving shocks are classic one-time cash flow hits.
teacherNote: Use this to compare reserves versus bridge debt.

title: Air-conditioner repair
story: A home cooling issue creates an urgent repair bill.
knowledgePoint: Not every important cost is monthly, but many are inevitable over time.
teacherNote: Useful for discussing maintenance budgeting.

title: Parents need support this month
story: Your family budget tightens and you are asked to contribute more.
knowledgePoint: Family support and reverse support are real cash flow variables for young adults.
teacherNote: Use salary-based scaling to show why the same event does not hit everyone equally.

title: Property rule fine
story: A parking or utility rule breach creates a small but annoying penalty.
knowledgePoint: Small compliance failures often become friction costs.
teacherNote: Show how preventable mistakes erode cash flow.

title: Rent top-up
story: Renewing your lease now requires a higher payment.
knowledgePoint: Some shocks raise future costs, not just one-time expenses.
teacherNote: Connect this with fixed cost lock and emergency months.

title: Phone screen repair
story: Your phone breaks and needs repair to stay usable.
knowledgePoint: Small equipment failures are common and should be planned for.
teacherNote: Good moment to distinguish maintenance from impulsive upgrades.

title: Laptop failure
story: Your laptop needs repair and delaying it affects productivity.
knowledgePoint: Some spending looks like consumption but is really preserving earning ability.
teacherNote: Connect this with device readiness and side income.

title: Lost accessory or device
story: A small but necessary electronic item goes missing during travel.
knowledgePoint: Replacement spending is not always vanity spending.
teacherNote: Useful for discussing replacement versus upgrade.

title: Traffic fine
story: A traffic mistake creates an unexpected fine.
knowledgePoint: Behavioral mistakes create friction costs that feel small but add up.
teacherNote: If the student owns a vehicle, this becomes a richer discussion.

title: Impulse purchase regret
story: A livestream purchase feels unnecessary as soon as it arrives.
knowledgePoint: Emotional spending is often visible only after the money leaves.
teacherNote: Good for discussing discipline and waiting periods.

title: Software renewal
story: A tool you rely on for study or work now needs a paid renewal.
knowledgePoint: Some subscriptions protect future earning capacity.
teacherNote: A nice bridge between tools, productivity, and side income.

title: Short video incentive
story: A content experiment unexpectedly earns a small platform reward.
knowledgePoint: Non-salary income often comes from earlier effort and experimentation.
teacherNote: Use this to discuss human capital and uneven payoff timing.

title: Referral side gig
story: A classmate introduces you to a small paid editing task.
knowledgePoint: Extra income is often limited by time and focus, not only opportunity.
teacherNote: Compare skill-based income with speculation.

title: Scholarship or contest bonus
story: Past effort in learning pays back through a cash reward.
knowledgePoint: Learning can have delayed but meaningful financial returns.
teacherNote: Tie this back to the learning module.

title: Tutoring opportunity
story: A friend introduces a short tutoring job that pays modestly.
knowledgePoint: Reliable small income can matter more than flashy risky wins.
teacherNote: Useful for explaining income stacking.

title: Client cancels part of a gig
story: A side client backs out and only part of the work gets paid.
knowledgePoint: Side income is not guaranteed and should not be treated as a certainty.
teacherNote: Discuss volatility in self-generated income.

title: Internship allowance
story: A short practical role gives you a stipend and confidence boost.
knowledgePoint: Career experience can improve both immediate and future earning power.
teacherNote: Good card for discussing salary growth versus market gains.

title: Wedding gift
story: A friend gets married and social norms push you to spend.
knowledgePoint: Human relationships create predictable but often under-budgeted spending.
teacherNote: Useful for discussing budget boundaries.

title: Class reunion
story: A reunion ends up costing more than expected.
knowledgePoint: Fun spending is not wrong, but it should still respect a plan.
teacherNote: Compare students with and without debt stress.

title: Marriage discussion begins
story: A relationship turns serious and spending conversations start to appear.
knowledgePoint: Life-stage planning often starts before the biggest payment arrives.
teacherNote: Connect this with engagement, wedding, and family support modules.

title: Anniversary spending
story: A special date tempts you into a celebration budget.
knowledgePoint: Emotional spending still competes with long-term goals.
teacherNote: Good moment to discuss planned versus reactive spending.

title: Relative asks for short-term help
story: A close relative requests temporary financial help.
knowledgePoint: Lending or supporting within family networks is never risk free.
teacherNote: Good card for talking about soft obligations and reserve planning.

title: Premium social event
story: A high-end networking or leisure invitation is expensive but hard to refuse.
knowledgePoint: Status spending does not automatically convert into financial return.
teacherNote: Use this to separate image costs from productive investment.

title: Fake task scam
story: A side-job message promises easy money but quickly asks for deposits.
knowledgePoint: Low-barrier high-return offers are often fraud rather than opportunity.
teacherNote: Connect with cyber protection and behavioral discipline.

title: Follow-trading trap
story: A group chat shows huge returns and pushes you to copy a trade.
knowledgePoint: Being led by someone else's screenshot is a red flag, not analysis.
teacherNote: Good bridge to high-risk exposure and crypto narratives.

title: Fake refund contact
story: A caller pretending to be platform support tries to freeze your attention and money.
knowledgePoint: Fraud loss prevention is a real financial skill.
teacherNote: Use this with cyber cover and safety setup.

title: Card misuse alert
story: A suspicious payment slips through before you lock the account.
knowledgePoint: Payment security is part of financial health, not just a tech topic.
teacherNote: Good card for cyber cover and safety setup.

title: Guaranteed platform pitch
story: An ad promises capital safety and unusually high returns at the same time.
knowledgePoint: Guaranteed high return claims deserve suspicion, not trust.
teacherNote: Tie this to product screening and fraud awareness.

title: Trusted contact investment trap
story: A familiar person recommends a so-called guaranteed project.
knowledgePoint: Relationship trust does not reduce investment risk.
teacherNote: Use this to explain affinity fraud and social proof.
